efficacyPairs.questionText: big (dig) → cat (__). The word in brackets is made by changing the first letter. What word goes in the brackets?
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **bat → cat**. Go through each letter position. When you find one that's different, you've found the rule.
lessonLearningGoal: Spot when one letter has been secretly swapped for another
Figure out WHICH position changes and what it changes to

efficacyPairs.questionText: Complete the third pair in the same way: (cold, old) (charm, harm) (chill, ___)
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **charm → harm**. Line up the letters side by side. The one that's gone tells you the rule — was it the first, second, or last letter?
lessonLearningGoal: Spot when a letter has vanished from the word — and figure out which one
Identify which position the letter was removed from

efficacyPairs.questionText: If each letter is moved forward by 1 place in the alphabet, what does FISH become?
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **CAT → DBU**. Use the alphabet line to count how many places the first letter moved. Then check the others match.
lessonLearningGoal: Spot when every letter has shifted forward or backward in the alphabet
Apply +1, +2, or -1 shifts confidently — one letter at a time

efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **charm → harm**. Line up the letters side by side. The one that's gone tells you the rule — was it the first, second, or last letter?
lessonLearningGoal: Spot when a letter has vanished from the word — and figure out which one

efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **STAR → RATS**. Start from the LAST letter of **STOP** and work backwards to the first. Write down each letter as you go.

Identify which position the letter was removed from

efficacyPairs.questionText: Look at the pattern: POOL → LOOP, KEEP → PEEK. What does MOOD become?
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Look at **STAR → RATS**. Start from the LAST letter of **STOP** and work backwards to the first. Write down each letter as you go.
lessonLearningGoal: Spot when a word has been flipped backwards — it's a common trick!
Reverse words of different lengths accurately every time
